programs.name: Medicaid for Seniors/Disabled
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage for low-income seniors and disabled individuals, covering doctor visits, hospital stays, and long-term care services. In Alaska, it is administered through the Department of Health's Division of Health Care Services.
programs.who_its_for: Alaska residents aged 65+ or disabled with low income and limited assets.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; covers eligible medical costs with no or low premiums.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers waiver services to allow seniors and disabled individuals to receive care at home instead of nursing homes. Includes personal care, homemaker services, and respite care tailored to Alaska's needs.
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors 65+ or disabled at risk of nursing home placement.
programs.benefit_value: Customized services up to institutional level of care.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health and transportation. In Alaska, availability is limited but follows federal PACE model.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified as nursing home eligible who can live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care coordinated by interdisciplinary team.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income seniors; QMB covers Part A/B costs, SLMB/QI cover Part B. Administered as Medicaid buy-in programs in Alaska.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries 65+ with income below federal limits (e.g., QMB ~100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: $0 Part B premium for QI (~$174.70/month value); full cost-sharing for QMB.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly EBT card benefits to buy food at stores. Alaska seniors get deductions for medical expenses over $35/month.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (higher deductions); income limit ~$1,580/month individual.

programs.name: Alaska Energy Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help pay heating and energy bills during winter months. Application period runs October to March.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; priority for elderly and disabled.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Funds home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and sealing to reduce heating costs. Prioritizes low-income homes including seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Alaska homeowners or renters 60+ with high energy burdens.
programs.benefit_value: Free services; average savings $300+/year on energy bills.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free counseling on Medicare options, supplements, and savings programs. Alaska's SHIP helps navigate choices year-round.
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, and family in Alaska.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; helps save thousands on premiums.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (Senior Meals Program)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors and funds congregate dining sites. Operated through Alaska's Aging Network.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals; income-based priority.

programs.name: Alzheimer's Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, training, and support for caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's or related dementias. Includes counseling and supplies.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of Alaska seniors 60+ with dementia.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,500/year per caregiver.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid work training at community sites leading to unsubsidized employment. Alaska hosts SCSEP through local sponsors.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ seeking work experience.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay (~$11.91/hr AK); 20 hrs/week training.

programs.name: Alaska Legal Services Corporation (ALSC) Senior Legal Hotline
programs.what_it_does: Offers free legal advice and representation for civil issues like benefits, housing, and abuse. Senior-specific hotline and clinics statewide.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ in Alaska facing legal problems.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services valued at $200+/hr attorney time.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities to resolve complaints and protect rights. Operated by Alaska's Division of Senior and Disabilities Services.
programs.who_its_for: Residents 65+ in long-term care facilities and their families in Alaska.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; resolves 80%+ of complaints without litigation.
